docs.title: Building Autonomous Robots From Scratch
docs.text:
Every chapter begins with a question and ends with something that moves.

Welcome!

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
You have invented the wheel.

It still doesn't move.

Why?

docs.title: Challenge
docs.text:
You have: - One wheel - A flat floor

How can motion begin?

docs.title: Exercise
docs.text: Sketch three different ways to make the wheel move before reading further.

docs.title: Philosophy
docs.text:
• Discover before defining.
  • Build before abstracting.
  • No black boxes.
  • Every chapter ends with code.

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text:
1. Why Wheels Move
  2. Why Things Refuse to Move
  3. Why Magnets Pull
  4. Why Electricity Can Push
  5. Why Copper Becomes a Magnet
  6. Why Motors Spin
  7. Why Motors Stop
  8. Why Brushes Had to Disappear
  9. Why Three Wires Are Better Than Two
  10. Why Motors Need Conductors
